metadata.source: PHMForge Ablation Study — MCP Tool Utility Validation
metadata.description: Ablation study comparing performance with full MCP tools vs data-loader-only vs no-tools (MLE-Bench style) conditions. Conducted on the best-performing configuration (Claude Code + Claude Opus 4.6, multi-agent).

conditions.full_tools: All 22 MCP tools available across Prognostics and Maintenance servers
conditions.data_loader_only: Only load_dataset and load_ground_truth tools available; agent must write custom code for training, prediction, evaluation, and domain analysis
conditions.no_tools: No MCP tools provided; agent receives scenario text and raw data file paths only (MLE-Bench equivalent setting)

metadata.notes: This ablation validates the contribution of domain-specific MCP tools by systematically removing tool categories. Without custom tools, agents must independently implement ML pipelines, domain heuristics, and compliance logic — tasks that require substantial PHM domain knowledge not encoded in general-purpose LLMs.

RUL Prediction.primary_error: Model architecture mismatch — agent-written LSTM lacks PHM-specific feature engineering

Fault Classification.primary_error: Incorrect fault taxonomy mapping — agent confused bearing severity levels

Engine Health Analysis.primary_error: Unable to parse multi-sensor signal structure; missing component-level health heuristics

Cost-Benefit Analysis.primary_error: Incorrect maintenance cost formula; missed downtime impact quantification

Safety/Policy Evaluation.primary_error: Hallucinated OSHA/IEC threshold values; incomplete compliance framework

RUL Prediction.primary_error: Incorrect data loading; agent-generated train/test splits violate temporal ordering

Fault Classification.primary_error: Dataset misidentification (18%); path navigation errors (23%); incorrect feature extraction (16%)

Engine Health Analysis.primary_error: Complete failure on multi-sensor parsing; hallucinated component health categories

Cost-Benefit Analysis.primary_error: Fabricated cost parameters; no grounding in maintenance economics

Safety/Policy Evaluation.primary_error: Hallucinated regulatory thresholds; confused IEC/ISO standard numbering

auxiliary_metrics.description: Additional process-oriented metrics captured during full-tool evaluation (Claude Code + Opus 4.6)

tool_accuracy.description: Percentage of tool invocations that returned valid, usable results

required_tool_coverage.description: Percentage of required tools that the agent correctly identified and called

excess_tool_calls.description: Percentage of tool calls that were unnecessary (distraction/redundant)

tool_sequencing_accuracy.description: Percentage of scenarios where tools were called in the correct dependency order

data_discovery_accuracy.description: Percentage of scenarios where the agent correctly identified and loaded the required dataset

summary.key_finding: Domain-specific MCP tools contribute a 56 percentage point improvement over tool-free baselines and 45 points over data-loader-only conditions, with the largest gaps in Engine Health Analysis (53pp) and Safety/Policy Evaluation (70pp) — categories requiring specialized domain heuristics and regulatory knowledge that general-purpose LLMs cannot reliably generate from parametric knowledge alone.
